explanations.7: The parameters in this constructor are declared with visibility modifiers (private), which means they automatically become class properties — you don't need separate $this->firstname = $firstname lines.
explanations.22: Returning $this allows you to chain method calls together, like $employee->setFirstName('Jane')->setLastName('Smith'), instead of calling each setter separately.
explanations.46: The semicolon after the closing brace is not needed for a class definition — it's legal but unusual in PHP.
